content: The Future of Coffee Is Here — And It Tastes Like a Forest
Meet the farmers reinventing single-origin sourcing from the ground up
For most of coffee history, origin stories were marketing. A farm name on a bag meant almost nothing about what was inside. That's changing fast.
The new generation of importers is using GPS-tagged micro-lots, blockchain provenance records, and blind cupping panels to bring traceability into every roast.
You can't hide behind vague country names anymore. The buyer knows which tree the cherry came from.
A coffee farmer checks ripeness at the Finca El Sol estate in Colombia
